variant_label: Land Use — Iranian population share living and working as tenant farmers under the land-tenure system
notes: Cited by the author to Daniel Craig, "The Impact of Land Reform on an Iranian Village," Middle East Journal 32, no. 2 (1978), p. 144. [cite: Jack Roush, "The Motheral Report and Land Reform in Iran, 1952-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2025, p.2]

variant_label: Land Use — Villages within the Shah's own landholdings ordered divided among the local population
notes: Official edict (firman) issued by the Shah, distributed via the Amlak agency without cost to recipients. [cite: Jack Roush, "The Motheral Report and Land Reform in Iran, 1952-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2025, p.3]

variant_label: Land Use — Villages targeted for land redistribution under the 1952 Point Four-incorporated program
notes: A pilot-project approach; program was scrapped by Prime Minister Mossadegh's rival legislation by early 1953. [cite: Jack Roush, "The Motheral Report and Land Reform in Iran, 1952-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2025, p.3]

variant_label: Land Use — Villages targeted for land redistribution under Motheral's proposed five-year program
notes: Proposed timeline from the 1957 Motheral Report; teams of US and Iranian technicians, transitioning to fully Iranian-staffed and US-advisory by end of period. [cite: Jack Roush, "The Motheral Report and Land Reform in Iran, 1952-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2025, p.7]

variant_label: Government Finance — Industrial Development Bank of Iran established (later Industrial and Mining Development Bank)
notes: Established as an offshoot of Iran's Bank Melli with private capital from both Iran and the US, urged by both governments as an expansion of development-banking activity called for in the Motheral Report. [cite: Jack Roush, "The Motheral Report and Land Reform in Iran, 1952-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2025, p.6]

variant_label: Government Finance — Central Bank of Iran established with consultation of US-educated economists
notes: Took an active role in financing development projects including land reform moving into the 1960s. [cite: Jack Roush, "The Motheral Report and Land Reform in Iran, 1952-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2025, p.6]

variant_label: Demographics & Population — Iranians the Shah claimed the White Revolution would "emancipate"
notes: The Shah's own framing/claim for the land-reform-centered White Revolution program, not an independently verified figure. [cite: Jack Roush, "The Motheral Report and Land Reform in Iran, 1952-1963" (Rockefeller Archive Center Research Reports), 2025, p.9]
